metadata.title: Why a Particle in a Box Cannot Sit Still

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. A quantum particle in a box cannot have zero energy the way a classical marble can. The wavefunction must vanish at both walls, forcing at least a half-wavelength arch. Any curved wavefunction carries momentum, and momentum means unavoidable kinetic energy. Tighter boxes demand sharper curvature, so confinement pushes the ground-state energy higher.

props.artifactTitle: Why the box floor isn't zero
artifactLines: A quantum particle in a box cannot have zero energy the way a classical marble can.
The wavefunction must vanish at both walls, forcing at least a half-wavelength arch.
Any curved wavefunction carries momentum, and momentum means unavoidable kinetic energy.
Tighter boxes demand sharper curvature, so confinement pushes the ground-state energy higher.

beats.narration_text: The part that clicked was that the wavefunction must hit zero at both walls, so the flattest allowed shape is still a half-wave arch, and any curve carries momentum hence energy. I want to nail the numbers. Please: (1) derive the ground-state energy from that half-wavelength boundary condition, (2) confirm the 0.38 electron-volt value for a 1-nanometer box, and (3) show how the energy scales as I shrink the box to 0.5 nanometers so I can see confinement pushing the floor up.

props.segment: Why a Particle in a Box Cannot Sit Still
props.command: The part that clicked was that the wavefunction must hit zero at both walls, so the flattest allowed shape is still a half-wave arch, and any curve carries momentum hence energy. I want to nail the numbers. Please: (1) derive the ground-state energy from that half-wavelength boundary condition, (2) confirm the 0.38 electron-volt value for a 1-nanometer box, and (3) show how the energy scales as I shrink the box to 0.5 nanometers so I can see confinement pushing the floor up.

props.segment: Why a Particle in a Box Cannot Sit Still
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: classical marble in a box rolling to the bottom and stopping — zero kinetic energy, perfectly still"

beats.narration_text: Drop a marble in a box — it rolls to the bottom and stops. Energy: zero. That's classical mechanics and it makes complete sense.
Now the quantum version. There's a box. A particle inside. Classical prediction: the lowest energy should be zero — just sit still. It doesn't. Why?

card.copy: Classical physics predicts E = 0 is allowed. The quantum particle's lowest energy is above zero. Why?

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Now the quantum version. There's a box. A particle inside. Classical prediction: the lowest energy should be zero — just sit still. It doesn't. Why?

props.segment: Why a Particle in a Box Cannot Sit Still
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: infinite square well walls — wavefunction must be exactly zero at both walls because the particle cannot exist there"

beats.narration_text: The wavefunction describes where the particle might be found. At both walls, the particle simply cannot exist — so the wavefunction must be exactly zero there.

props.segment: Why a Particle in a Box Cannot Sit Still
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: half-wavelength arch fitting between the walls — simplest wavefunction satisfying zero boundary conditions, one bump of a sine wave"

beats.narration_text: Zero at both ends. The wavefunction starts at zero on the left wall, rises somewhere in the middle, and must return to zero at the right wall.

props.segment: Why a Particle in a Box Cannot Sit Still
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: minimum arch shape in the box — half-sine, cannot be flatter without violating boundary conditions at both walls"

beats.narration_text: The simplest curve that does this is a half-wavelength — one arch of a sine wave. That's the ground state. It is the minimum possible shape.

props.segment: Why a Particle in a Box Cannot Sit Still
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: wavefunction curvature means momentum — curved wavefunction has de Broglie wavelength, curvature proportional to momentum squared proportional to kinetic energy"

beats.narration_text: Now here is the key: a curved wavefunction means the particle has momentum. A flatter curve means less — but the curve cannot go flat. It still has to arch from zero to zero.

props.segment: Why a Particle in a Box Cannot Sit Still
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: confinement forces energy up — plot E1 = h^2/(8mL^2) vs box width L, as L shrinks E1 rises steeply"

beats.narration_text: Momentum means kinetic energy. The tighter the box, the sharper the minimum arch, the more curvature, the higher the ground state energy. Confinement forces the energy up.

props.segment: Why a Particle in a Box Cannot Sit Still
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: flat wavefunction means no particle — psi = 0 everywhere gives zero probability of finding anything, the particle does not exist"

beats.narration_text: What if the wavefunction were flat — a horizontal line at zero? That would mean zero energy. But a flat line through zero is no particle at all — the probability of finding it anywhere would be zero.

props.segment: Why a Particle in a Box Cannot Sit Still
props.command: claude "write a Manim scene: 1 nm box ground state worked example — E1 approximately 0.38 eV, label this on an energy diagram with visible light photon energies for scale"

beats.narration_text: Illustrative example: a 1-nanometer box. The ground state energy works out to about 0.38 electron-volts — visible light carries around 2 eV. That gap is real and measurable.
The ground state cannot vanish — not because of any extra assumption, but because the boundary conditions demand a curve, and any curve carries energy. The zero is unreachable.

card.copy: The wavefunction must arch. Any arch is momentum. Momentum is energy. Zero is forbidden.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The ground state cannot vanish — not because of any extra assumption, but because the boundary conditions demand a curve, and any curve carries energy. The zero

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Five box sizes. Ask Claude to calculate and plot the ground state energy for each. Watch E1 rise as the box shrinks.

props.segment: Why a Particle in a Box Cannot Sit Still
props.command: for a 1D particle in a box of width L, plot the ground state energy E1 = h^2/(8mL^2) for L = 0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, and 5 nanometers — show how confinement drives E1 up as L shrinks

beats.narration_text: Why a Particle in a Box Cannot Sit Still
